coping_questions.prompt: When something is bothering you, what is your usual first move?

options.label: I scan for what might go wrong and prepare
I focus on keeping everyone else okay first
I push the feeling down and carry on
I handle it alone; I don't expect help

coping_questions.prompt: When someone is upset with you, you tend to...

options.label: Replay it over and over, watching for danger
Apologize quickly, even if unsure it's my fault
Go quiet and avoid the conversation
Feel it threatens everything and pull back

coping_questions.prompt: How do you usually relate to rest and downtime?

options.label: I feel guilty or restless when I'm not productive
I can't relax until everyone else is taken care of
I rest only when I've 'earned' it
I find it hard to switch off; I stay alert

coping_questions.prompt: When you achieve something, how does it feel?

options.label: Good briefly, then I'm onto the next thing
Like relief more than joy
I downplay it; it wasn't a big deal
I worry it raises expectations I must keep meeting

coping_questions.prompt: In close relationships, you most often...

options.label: Keep a part of myself guarded
Put their needs ahead of my own
Avoid bringing up things that might cause friction
Feel responsible for their wellbeing

coping_questions.prompt: When strong emotions come up, you...

options.label: Analyze them rather than feel them
Worry they'll overwhelm me, so I contain them
Channel them into doing or fixing something
Stay watchful of how others are reacting

coping_questions.prompt: How often do you feel 'on guard,' even when things are calm?

options.label: Often
Sometimes
Rarely
Rarely ask; I'd rather not depend on anyone
Feel I should be able to manage alone
Worry about being a burden
Ask easily
